summary: TZ display fix correct: override>configured>browser>NY, override still wins, Promise.all res[4] wiring verified. 2 WARN: unset fallback diverges from TZ-DISPLAY-3 (browser-not-NY) + dead under DB default; undefined-guard staleness.

findings.claim: Unset-user display fallback resolves to browser before NY, diverging from documented TZ-DISPLAY-3 (NY default); branch is also largely dead because users.timezone has a non-null DB default of America/New_York.
findings.evidence: resolveDisplayTimezone returns o.override||o.userTimezone||o.browser||DEFAULT_TIMEZONE; TIMEZONE-RULES.md:50-51 (TZ-DISPLAY-3) says unset => America/New_York not browser; 20260301000000_initial_schema.js:13 'timezone' defaultTo('America/New_York') so getUserTimezone returns NY for unset prod users, never null, making the browser tier unreachable for real rows.
findings.fix: Either align unset fallback to NY (drop browser tier) per TZ-DISPLAY-3, or record a decision superseding TZ-DISPLAY-3 for the display path. Confirm no real row has NULL timezone; if none, browser tier is dead code.

findings.claim: localStorage USER_TZ_KEY sync is gated on config.userTimezone !== undefined; a payload that omits the field leaves a stale key, so non-React readers keep a wrong configured tz.
findings.evidence: if (config.userTimezone !== undefined) { setUserTimezone(...); set/removeItem(USER_TZ_KEY) }. set-vs-remove branch is correct (truthy set, falsy remove); the undefined-guard is the gap. Mitigated: GetConfig.js always emits userTimezone (null when unset), so it is never undefined from the current backend.
findings.fix: Acceptable as-is (backend always sends the key). If the field could ever be dropped, clear USER_TZ_KEY on absence rather than skipping.

findings.claim: Empty-string userTimezone correctly treated as unset (removeItem + falls through resolver).
findings.evidence: else branch removeItem(USER_TZ_KEY) on falsy ''; resolveDisplayTimezone '' || ... skips it. Consistent and intended.

findings.claim: userTimezone is cached in the 1h-TTL config result; a tz change is not reflected until cache invalidation, same window as all config fields (not new to this diff).
findings.evidence: result includes userTimezone then cache.set(cacheKey, result, 3600).
findings.fix: REFER->telly for cache-invalidation-on-tz-change coverage if absent.

refers.why: Confirm coverage of backend getUserTimezone + useConfig localStorage-sync paths and cache-invalidation-on-tz-change; ernie does not count coverage.
Product/UX call: should a brand-new unconfigured user see browser-local or NY task times (the W1 unset-fallback semantics).
